species.citation: Benth. , London J. Bot. 2 (1843) 215 ; Leenhouts, Fl. Males ser. 1, 5 (1956) 293; Cortez et al., Check List 19 (2023) 987; Romeroso et al., J. Asia-Pacific Biodiv. 17 (2024) 10. ssp. asperum. = Canarium sibuyanense Elmer, LPB 4 (1912) 1502. Distribution: Borneo, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Sulawesi. BALABAC, CEBU, LAGEN, LEYTE, LUZON: Aurora, Benguet, Isabela, Laguna, Rizal, Zambales, MASBATE, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Davao del Sur, Surigao del Sur, MINDORO, PALAWAN, SAMAR, SIBUYAN. Native. var. asperum. = Canarium villosum Benth. & Hook.f. ex Náves, Fl. Filip., 3rd Ed. 4(Nov. App.) (1880) 40; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 354. = Canarium minutiflorum Engl., Monogr. Phan. 4 (1883) 123; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 351. = Canarium calophyllum Perkins, Fragm. Fl. Philipp. (1904) 91; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 349. = Canarium lucidum Perkins, Fragm. Fl. Philipp. (1904) 94; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 351. = Canarium polyneurum Perkins, Fragm. Fl. Philipp. (1904) 96; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 353. = Canarium reticulatum Merr., PJS 3c (1908) 141; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 353. = Canarium apoense Elmer, LPB 3 (1911) 1083; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 349. = Canarium ogat Elmer, LPB 3 (1911) 1086; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 352. = Canarium euphlebium Merr., PJS 8c (1913) 372; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 350. = Canarium heterophyllum Merr., PJS 10c (1915) 19; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 350. = Canarium lagunense Merr., PJS 10c (1915) 21; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 350. = Canarium barnesii Merr., PJS 10c (1915) 22; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 349. = Canarium sanchezii Merr., PJS 10c (1915) 27; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 353. = Canarium agusanense Elmer, LPB 7 (1915) 2564; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 349. = Canarium urdanetense Elmer, LPB 7 (1915) 2568; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 354. = Canarium samarense Merr., PJS 11c (1916) 183; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 353. = Canarium unifoliolatum Merr., PJS 17 (1921) 267; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 354. Distribution: Borneo, Lesser Sunda Isls, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Sulawesi. PALAWAN, SIBUYAN. Notes: Throughout the Philippines. Common in primary and secondary forests on both seasonal and ever-wet areas, also in more open savanna-like sites, on various soil types including waterlogged. 0-1000(-1800)m. Native. var. clementis (Merr.) Leenh., Fl. Males., 1st Ser. 5 (1956) 295; ≡ Canarium clementis Merr., PJS 3c (1908) 142. = Canarium leytense Elmer, LPB 3 (1911) 1081, nom. inval., nom. nud. = Canarium wenzelii Merr., PJS 9c (1914) 363; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 355. Distribution: MINDANAO: Bukidnon. Native.

Bur. Sci. Gov. Lab. 35 (1906) 26. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. CATANDUANES, CEBU (photos), LUZON: Camarines Sur, Isabela, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Davao del Sur, MINDORO, NEGROS (photos), PALAWAN, PANAY, SIBUYAN. Notes: Throughout the Philippines except Palawan. Rather common in lowland forests. 0-800(-1700)m. Native. var. ramosii (Merr.) Leenh., Fl. Males., 1st Ser. 5 (1956) 280; Fernando et al., Forest Flora of Dinagat (2017) 31; ≡ Canarium ramosii Merr., PJS 8c (1913) 374; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 353; Type: Ramos 1168, Leyte: Dagami, Aug-1912. = Canarium ramosii var. parvum Merr., PJS 8c (1913) 375; Type: Wenzel 103, Leyte: Dagami, 12-Jun-1913. = Canarium paucinervium Merr., PJS 9c (1914) 364; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 352. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. BUCAS GRANDE, DINAGAT, LEYTE, MINDANAO: Surigao del Norte, SAMAR. Notes: Low elevation forests. Native.
